Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper repeatedly places OsGOGAT1 in nitrogen assimilation and the GS/GOGAT cycle.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results and Discussion support roles in ammonium assimilation and nitrogen remobilization.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: OsGOGAT1 is clearly part of assimilation, but 'midstream' is an interpretive label not used in the paper.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The Discussion mentions the GS/GOGAT cycle and glutamine/glutamate balance, but the exact reaction wording with 2-oxoglutarate and NADH is not explicitly stated in the paper text.

Primary_Product.reason: Glutamate is discussed as part of the GS/GOGAT cycle, but not explicitly as the direct product of OsGOGAT1 in the Results.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results show improved N remobilization and increased grain nitrogen/protein/amino acids in OsGOGAT1 activation lines, supporting connection to grain nutrient accumulation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Discussion explicitly states that GOGAT activity is the bottleneck of the GS/GOGAT cycle.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly measure increased grain nitrogen, protein, and amino acids in OsGOGAT1 activation lines.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Measured endpoints are nitrogen, amino acids, and protein, matching this class description.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links higher grain N/protein to improved nutritional quality and shows better performance under N limitation, especially in the double line.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results support grain accumulation and movement through flag leaves/newly emerging leaves during remobilization assays.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Discussion cites prior reports that OsGOGAT1 is important for primary ammonium assimilation in roots and reutilization in developing organs.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Activation-tagging increased OsGOGAT1 transcript at seedling stage, but the paper does not report OsGOGAT1 expression specifically in flag leaves; the improved remobilization association is supported, the expression statement is only partial.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed conditions/treatments are directly described: N limitation, paddy field, outdoor low-N pots, 15N assays, hydroponics with ammonium.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: OsGS1;1 and OsGS1;2 expression/activity changes alongside OsGOGAT1 activation, but direct protein interaction is not tested.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support enhanced low-N tolerance, improved remobilization, higher grain N/protein/amino acids in single OsGOGAT1 lines, and improved low-N yield only in combination with OsAMT1;2.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly used in the paper.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are supported by Results, with the yield improvement specifically applying to the double activation line.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Summary/Discussion propose combining OsGOGAT1 with OsAMT1;2 as a breeding strategy to improve NUE and grain quality while avoiding single-line penalties.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show single OsGOGAT1 activation lines had reduced height, reduced spikelet number/fertility, reduced grain dimensions/seed weight, and lower productivity in paddy fields.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results clearly show stronger effects of OsGOGAT1 on N remobilization and that single-gene activation improves grain quality but not field productivity under prolonged low N.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Ammonium/NH4+ is explicitly the substrate tested and discussed.

Primary_Product.reason: Higher root ammonium concentrations are measured, but 'intracellular ammonium pool' is an interpretive formulation rather than explicit wording.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These measured endpoints fit nitrogenous metabolites/amino acids/protein.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states enhanced grain nutritional quality and improved low-N performance particularly when OsAMT1;2 is combined with OsGOGAT1.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Root ammonium uptake and final grain N/protein enrichment are both directly measured.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper states expression in the central cylinder and cell surface of root tips, but does not directly determine subcellular localization as plasma membrane.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Discussion explicitly cites prior work that OsAMT1;2 is expressed in the central cylinder and cell surface of root tips and is ammonium-inducible.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Discussion states activation tagging maintained root-specific expression while enhancing expression level.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed factors/treatments are directly used in the study.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: OsGS1;1 and OsGS1;2 are induced in OsAMT1;2 activation lines, but no direct protein interaction is shown.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support increased ammonium uptake, better seedling low-N tolerance, higher grain N/protein/amino acids, and synergy with OsGOGAT1 for low-N yield improvement.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed validation methods are explicitly reported.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each summary statement is directly supported by the Results and Discussion, with superior NUE/low-N yield applying to the combined activation line.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly argues that combining OsAMT1;2 with OsGOGAT1 is a promising breeding strategy to avoid imbalance and improve NUE.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results document lower field biomass, reduced spikelet number and fertility, smaller grains/seed weight, earlier senescence, and no yield improvement under prolonged low N in single OsAMT1;2 lines.
